question: Which phrase means 'to lose control of your emotions and get angry'?
My uncle _____ always take me swimming when I was young. (repeated past action)
Which adjective describes someone who 'always tells the truth and acts with integrity'?
Read: 'A striking example of this is the humble washing machine, which Hans Rosling called the greatest invention of the industrial revolution.' Why did he call it the greatest invention?
choices: because it cleaned clothes faster than people could
because it made clothes look new
because it freed parents to spend more time with their children
because it saved families a lot of money

question: He didn't _____ to lose his temper so often when he was younger.
Which phrase means 'to view someone as a bad model whose behaviour negatively influences others'?
The architect of the tower, _____ began work in 2014, was very talented.
Which phrase means 'to stop being angry with someone and become friends again'?
Read: 'Jack introduced Oliver to an old man called Fagin, who gave Oliver some food and a bed to sleep on.' Why do you think Fagin gave Oliver food and a bed?
choices: because Fagin was a generous and kind person
because he wanted Oliver to trust him so he could use him later
because Fagin was the manager of a workhouse

question: In which sentence can the relative pronoun NOT be omitted?
choices: The book (that) I read was excellent.
The man (who) I met was kind.
Amman, which is the capital, is beautiful.
The app (that) you can keep in your pocket is useful.

question: Which phrase describes the act of 'moving to live in a different town or country'?
choices: a clash between you and a sibling
relocating to a different town or country
a row with your best friend

question: She _____ be the most creative student in the class, but now she doesn't show much interest.
What is a 'topic sentence' in academic writing?
choices: the last sentence of a paragraph
the sentence that contains the main idea of the paragraph
a sentence that gives background information
a sentence that concludes the essay

question: I found the book _____ page was torn at the library.
Which phrase means 'to suddenly start crying'?
Aunt Abeer has always wanted to help children and she is a really _____ person.

Read: 'Whereas in the past family members living apart would have to write letters and often wait a long time for a reply, nowadays there are many more ways to keep in touch.' This comparison shows that technology has:
choices: made family communication easier and more frequent
caused families to argue more often

question: My sister _____ always mess about in class when she was young.
Which word means 'to worship or have extreme admiration for someone'?
My grandma's house, _____ has no Wi-Fi, is very peaceful.
She _____ always be the last one to leave the office. (typical annoying present behaviour)
Which phrase means 'a person who inspires others through their achievements and behaviour'?
Read: 'In some cases, young people communicate more through their phones than face-to-face.' 'In some cases' is used to:
The shop _____ I bought my computer from has closed down.
Which phrase means 'to dismiss or feel contempt for someone'?
Huda is in a lot of pain from her illness, but she doesn't complain. She's _____.
In 'By and large, young people do more sport', 'by and large' means:
What does 'skinny' mean?
These headphones, _____ I paid a fortune for, stopped working after two days.
Read: 'Oliver is adopted by Mr Brownlow, which shows what a kind man he is.' The word 'which' here refers to:
choices: the whole idea of adopting Oliver

question: Which phrase means 'to have a strong disagreement'?
When we were kids, my sister _____ always losing her shoes. It drove my mum crazy.
Which phrase means 'to have an inspiring effect on people around you'?
Read: 'It is commonly accepted that the time needed to run a household has decreased.' This phrase introduces:
choices: a generalisation accepted by most people

question: Rewrite: 'The flip phone, which was designed in the 1990s, is rapidly becoming popular again.' (Participle clause)
choices: The flip phone, designing in the 1990s, is rapidly becoming popular again.
The flip phone, designed in the 1990s, is rapidly becoming popular again.
The flip phone, which designing in the 1990s, is rapidly becoming popular again.
The flip phone design in the 1990s is rapidly becoming popular again.

question: Which phrase means 'to genuinely share and understand another person's emotions'?
My sister _____ constantly winding me up when we were growing up together.
Which adjective means 'genuinely caring about others who are suffering or in need'?
The writer, _____ book was published last year, is very famous.
What is the correct structure of an opinion essay in order?
choices: main body then introduction then conclusion
conclusion then main body then introduction
introduction then main body then conclusion
introduction then conclusion then main body
